📖 How to Use TeachMate

🧭 Getting Around

Everything you need is on the buttons under your name on the home screen. Tap any one of them to open that feature:

  • 📋 Attendance Register — mark and view daily attendance per class. Fully yours to edit.
  • 🏫 Class Management — add students, edit class rosters. Fully yours to edit.
  • 📊 Results Tracking — enter, save, and review test/exam results, then generate report cards from them. Fully yours to edit.
  • 💰 Fees Tracking (ZMW) — view-only. You can see exactly what each student owes, what they've paid, the due date, and which months are covered — but the total fee amount, due date, and payments themselves are entered by your school's administrator, not here. This is intentional: it keeps one single source of truth for money records.
  • 📚 Lesson Planner — plan lessons (appears once your account has access).
  • 💬 Messages — communicate with parents/guardians.

📄 Generating Report Cards

Inside Results Tracking you have two ways to generate a report card, both producing the exact same professional PDF layout:

  • Export Student PDF — one student at a time. You review and can edit their subjects, scores, and both comments before generating.
  • 📚 Bulk Generate Report Cards — generates one for every student in the class automatically, one after another. You enter the shared details once (school logo, teacher name/phone, category, level), and it pulls each student's own scores and comments from what's already saved — using any comment you've personally edited before, or a fresh auto-generated one if you haven't. Students with no saved results yet are skipped, not force-generated with blank data. Your browser may ask permission to allow multiple downloads during bulk generation — approve it once so every file can save.

🔒 Premium Features

Inside Results, you'll see 🤖 Auto Mark, 📲 Send Results via SMS, 📄 Export Student PDF, and 📚 Bulk Generate Report Cards. If any of these show a small lock icon, it means your school administrator hasn't switched that feature on for your school yet — tap it to see the message, and ask your administrator directly if you need it enabled. This is a school-level setting, not something tied to you personally.

🏢 How TeachMate Fits Together

TeachMate is actually a small family of apps, each with a clear job, all reading from the same live school data:

  • This app (TeachMate 3.0) — for teachers. Attendance, classes, results, report cards, lesson planning, messaging, and viewing (not editing) fees.
  • The Admin Dashboard — used by your school's administrator. Sets fee amounts and due dates, records parent payments, and handles deeper school administration. This is why fees appear here as view-only.
  • The Headteacher Dashboard — used by your headteacher. Approves new teacher accounts, and oversees school-wide data across every teacher and class.

If something feels like it's "missing" here — like editing a fee amount, or approving a new colleague's account — it's very likely simply living in one of those other apps by design, not lost.

🏫 How Class Access Actually Works

This trips people up more than anything else, so here's exactly how it works:

  • Who assigns YOU to a class: Your headteacher does this, from the Headteacher Dashboard — not the school administrator, and not something you can set yourself. They maintain the official list of which classes each teacher is responsible for. If a class you teach isn't showing up here, ask your headteacher to check your assignment — it's almost always a quick fix on their end.
  • Who gets YOUR assignment to actually show up: Once your headteacher assigns you, it should appear automatically next time you open the app or refresh. If it doesn't appear right away, it's usually just syncing — give it a moment with an internet connection, or log out and back in.
  • How students land in a class: Students get a classId on their record either by a teacher enrolling them directly (inside 🏫 Class Management here in this app), or through a bulk import done by the school administrator (used when onboarding a whole school's worth of student data at once). Either way, once a student has a classId, every teacher assigned to that same class sees them immediately — there's no separate "adding a student to your view" step.
  • Sharing a class with other teachers: More than one teacher can be assigned to the exact same class at the same time — completely normal for subject-based teaching, e.g. a Math teacher and an English teacher both assigned to "Form 1 Blue." Everyone assigned sees the same student roster, and each of you enters data for your own subject independently — nobody overwrites anybody else's work. When a colleague sharing your class syncs new attendance, results, or fees data, you'll get a real-time notification naming exactly who did what, so you always know what's changed even if you weren't the one who entered it.

✋ Gestures & Shortcuts

  • Double-tap a student's photo to open it full-size.
  • Pinch with two fingers on an opened photo to zoom in/out, and drag with one finger to move around while zoomed in.
  • Press and drag the 🌗 light/dark mode button to move it anywhere on your screen — it stays there next time you open the app.
  • Tap the "Tap to view your login email" pill on the home screen any time you forget which email you registered with — tap again to hide it.

🔄 Staying in Sync

TeachMate saves your work on your device first, then syncs it automatically when you have internet — you can keep working even with no signal, and it'll catch up once you're back online. Look for "All Data Synced ✓" at the bottom of the screen to confirm everything's saved.

🆘 Still Stuck?

Contact your school administrator or headteacher — they manage account approvals, fee records, and feature access for your school.

📂 Upload Your Data File
Works with any spreadsheet, exported list, or register — regardless of how columns are named. "Project Owner", "Candidate", "Learner" all work.
🗂️
Tap to select a file
Or drop it here on desktop.
All data is processed locally — nothing uploads until you confirm.
xlsx xls csv tsv txt ods pdf docx
🔒 File processed locally in your browser
🔬 Extraction Workspace
All data extracted exactly as-is. Select rows → assign class → continue. Tap any cell to edit. Use the toolbar to search or filter.
👆 Tap rows to select · Assign a class to continue
🗂️ Column Mapping
Tell TeachMate what each column means. AI suggestions shown — correct anything wrong. Your corrections are remembered next time. 🤖
⚠️ Map at least a Student Name (or First + Last Name) to continue.
💡 Corrections are saved per school.
🎯 Data Assignment Studio
Each imported row is matched to a student in your class. Attendance, results, and fee data are shown per student. Correct any mismatches using the dropdowns.

👁️ Preview & Edit
Review each student card. Edit any field before importing.
